FORM.HEADING: Join the Association / Request Information:
FORM.DESCRIPTION: The association's main purpose is to promote and highlight the industrial and professional fabric within the construction sector, the companies and organizations working to define a new model of industrialized construction (off-site, prefabricated, sustainable and meeting circular economy criteria), fostering competitiveness and collaboration among member associates and infrastructure within the construction sector.

FOOTER.DIRECTION: Av. Constituyentes 1, Historic Center, 76000 Querétaro, Mexico.

NEWSLETTER.TITLE: Welcome to the Future of Construction
NEWSLETTER.SUBTITLE: Be part of the change. Connect. Transform. Build.
NEWSLETTER.BODY:
Clúster de Construcción Industrializada de México is not just a business network: it is the hub where those redesigning the sector converge. Being part of the ClusterCIIM means joining an active community of innovation, where experience meets disruption and real opportunities for collaboration, visibility and growth open up.

Here, connections are forged with leading companies in the sector alongside a diverse range of SMEs and visionary professionals. We are rethinking every construction process for industrialization, seeking to improve efficiency and sustainability. From new products and modular systems to algorithms that move faster than regulations, the ecosystem advances… and you can be part of it.

If you believe that industrialization, data and applied intelligence can transform the way we build, you are in the right place. If you are looking to add capabilities, share projects or scale your impact, join today.

Pre-register, and I will get in touch with you and send you detailed information to access our network.

SECTIONS.TITLE: Industrialization
SECTIONS.DESCRIPTION: Any stakeholder, company, association, or professional who wants to contribute to the process of improving and modernizing the sector. Not only industrial companies, but also suppliers, SMEs, or professionals who, through their experience, products, or solutions, can contribute to the common goal.

SECTIONS.TITLE: Digitalization
SECTIONS.DESCRIPTION: This layer of applications applied to a sector traditionally resistant to digital technology. Adding sensors to buildings, facilities, or any construction to evolve the concept of home automation and bring it up to date with available technology.

SECTIONS.TITLE: Deep Digitalization
SECTIONS.DESCRIPTION: Within the transformation process driven by ClusterCIIM, there is an emerging vision we call deep digitalization, summarized in the MAIA concept: Modular + Algorithms + Artificial Intelligence. This approach allows rethinking the construction lifecycle from the ground up, connecting design, manufacturing, operation, commercialization, and management through intelligent data flows and adaptive modular structures. Based on advances in algorithmic design and procedural co-creation platforms, it shows how processes are not only optimized, but also open the door to a more evolutionary, connected, and resilient construction model.

SECTIONS.TITLE: Sustainability
SECTIONS.DESCRIPTION: Applying the latest trends and solutions to create passive buildings, or even energy-positive ones. We must act as evangelists for new green energy and reduced consumption. Construction may be the sector with the greatest potential for improvement in carbon footprint, circular economy, and waste reduction.

SECTIONS.TITLE: Training
SECTIONS.DESCRIPTION: A new way of building requires a new way of teaching. Trades evolve from those we already know, while new ones emerge. Professionals' knowledge must evolve with the market and new realities. That is why training is a crucial element in the sector’s transformation. We aim to collaborate with universities, vocational training centers, schools, and companies to define and educate the next generation of professionals.

CO_FACTORY.TITLE: Co-Factory
CO_FACTORY.SUBTITLE: We drive the industrialization of construction

CO_FACTORY_CONTENT.TITLE:
Asset Investment
Operations Investment
CO_FACTORY_CONTENT.DESCRIPTION:
We foster a collaborative space with SMEs from the ClusterCIIM, startups and industry professionals. Our public-private collaboration is essential for business development.

At ClusterCIIM we do not work only on short-term solutions; we have created a collaboration and debate tool to find the solutions the sector needs as it undergoes transformation.

CO_SHARE.TITLE: Co-Share
CO_SHARE.SUBTITLE: The cluster organizes bimonthly events with partners at iconic venues, fostering network creation.

CO_SHARE_CONTENT.TITLE: Co-Share
CO_SHARE_CONTENT.DESCRIPTION:
In that manifesto, the Federal Government of Mexico and the Government of the State of Querétaro set themselves a challenge:

'To create a BIM Working Group to help implement the culture, values, working methods, digital and electronic technologies to empower the industry, public administration, research centers, professional associations and academia'.

Since then, the Construimos el Futuro Commission has oriented its activities in this direction.

CO_MENTOR_CONTENT.TITLE: Co-Mentor
CO_MENTOR_CONTENT.DESCRIPTION: We support companies that are in the midst of an organizational transition, drawing on the experience of Cluster members who have driven disruptive change.

TEAM.TITLE: A great team
TEAM.DESCRIPTION:
Clúster de Construcción Industrializada de México (ClusterCIIM) was born as a coordinated response by the country's leading public and private stakeholders to drive a stable space for debate, action and real transformation of the construction sector. With the support of key institutions such as the Chamber of Commerce of Mexico, the National Business Council and the Government of Mexico, the ClusterCIIM has become the meeting point where the master lines of the country's constructive future are defined.

Since its inception, the cluster has oriented its activity toward the deep modernization of processes, with special attention to the integration of digital technologies, new organizational models and industrialized systems. This collective commitment to structural transformation — grounded in innovation, sustainability and collaboration — has made the ClusterCIIM the benchmark space for all initiatives seeking to rethink how the habitat of the future is designed, built and managed.

DIRECTORS.NAME: Héctor Miranda Martínez
DIRECTORS.SEMBLANCE: Expert in road construction, with extensive experience in planning, execution, and quality control of infrastructure projects in public and industrial sectors.

DIRECTORS.NAME: Osvaldo Mireles
DIRECTORS.SEMBLANCE: Expert in technology and sustainable development, specialized in Smart Cities, BIM, Blockchain, AI, and the New Urban Agenda.

DIRECTORS.NAME: Jorge Moreno Vargas
DIRECTORS.SEMBLANCE: Expert in disruptive technologies, MIT Certified. 13 years leading innovation in smart cities, IoT, and sustainable projects.

DIRECTORS.NAME: Teresita de Jesus Guerrero Escamilla
DIRECTORS.SEMBLANCE: Civil Engineer, graduated from the Autonomous University of Querétaro in 2004. Her academic background and career reflect her commitment to engineering and the development of civil projects.

TRANSFORMATION_OF_THE_CONSTRUCTION_SECTO.TITLE: Transformation of the Construction Sector
TRANSFORMATION_OF_THE_CONSTRUCTION_SECTO.DESCRIPTION:
Clúster de Construcción Industrializada de México (ClusterCIIM) was born from the union of professionals with solid and complementary backgrounds: from health applied to housing to architectural innovation, applied engineering, energy efficiency and industrial management; this strategic alliance allows us to shape a space of transformation.

We champion a new construction model based on coordination, digitalization and efficiency, using algorithmic methodologies alongside existing BIM and CAD tools, which enables the integration of customized industrialized processes for each company from concept to key handover.

The world is digital and automated; procedural co-creation platforms that explore the intersection of construction systems and details, technology, design, sustainability, marketing and monitoring are tools that bring new perspectives on deep changes in the way we build.

With this convergence of visions, we consolidate ourselves as a key player in leading the paradigm shift in the construction sector in Mexico, with an open, integrative outlook prepared to face the challenges of an ever-evolving world.

CONEXION_CREATING_COLLABORATIONS.TITLE: Connection, Creating Collaborations
CONEXION_CREATING_COLLABORATIONS.DESCRIPTION:
We act as a connector within the industrialized construction ecosystem, creating spaces where companies, professionals, public administrations and knowledge centers can come together to share challenges, visions and strategies. Thanks to the diversity and experience of its members, an active network of trust is built that facilitates the emergence of real collaborations.

These connections become tangible partnerships, shared projects and strategic alliances that allow complex challenges to be tackled from a collective approach. We build a bridge between innovation and action, fostering a collaborative culture in which different perspectives converge toward a single purpose: transforming the sector into a more efficient, sustainable and future-ready form of construction.

TECHNOLOGICAL_IMPLEMENTATION.TITLE: Technological Implementation
TECHNOLOGICAL_IMPLEMENTATION.SUBTITLE: Success Story
TECHNOLOGICAL_IMPLEMENTATION.DESCRIPTION:
Success story: Collaboration between various companies in the sector and a computational design studio specializing in parametric geometries. The goal: to develop a modular system for sustainable housing, adaptable to different contexts. Combining industrialized processes with generative algorithms, multiple design variants were generated and optimized according to local data such as climate, materials and culture.

The results were remarkable: a 70% reduction in design time and a 50% reduction in construction waste. The model, now replicated across several municipalities in Mexico, demonstrates how the combination of advanced manufacturing, digitalization and algorithmic design accelerates innovation and fosters collaboration among diverse teams, under a shared logic based on data and efficiency.
